channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Orbital-hybridization-induced Ising-type superconductivity in a confined gallium layer
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 13 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02573-y

Orbital hybridization and quantum confinement are used to realize Ising-like superconductivity in trilayers of gallium, which alone is probably not strongly enough spin–orbit coupled to host such superconductivity under high in-plane magnetic fields.
item.dc:title: Orbital-hybridization-induced Ising-type superconductivity in a confined gallium layer

item.title: All-van der Waals microcavities for low-loss nonlinear photonics
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 13 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02574-x

Photonic components fabricated from van der Waals materials and patterned using focused ion beam lithography with aluminium passivation demonstrate quality factors exceeding 106 and an efficient nonlinear optical response.
item.dc:title: All-van der Waals microcavities for low-loss nonlinear photonics
item.dc:creator: Zhi-Yan Wang
Xiaoqi Cui

item.title: Two-dimensional materials for integrated sensing
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 06 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02551-4

Two-dimensional (2D) materials are promising for in situ encoding and optoelectronic sensing in computing technologies. This Review discusses integrated optoelectronic sensing based on 2D materials and future challenges and prospects for integrated sensing encoding information beyond optical signals.
item.dc:title: Two-dimensional materials for integrated sensing
item.dc:creator: Hangyu Xu
Zhihao Xu
Qinqi Ren
Yuan Meng
Sangmoon Han
Zhaoqing Wang
Yang Chai
Sang-Hoon Bae
Weida Hu

item.title: Bulk nano-heterointerface secures molecular contacts in perovskite solar cells
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 06 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02546-1

The weak contact between the oxide electrode and molecular layer in inverted perovskite solar cells is prone to deformation, limiting long-term device stability. A bulk nano-heterointerface is designed to strengthen the binding force between molecules and spherical silicon oxide nanoparticles, increasing the interfacial contact area and improving the operational stability of perovskite solar cells.
item.dc:title: Bulk nano-heterointerface secures molecular contacts in perovskite solar cells
item.dc:creator: Yixin Luo

item.title: Hydrogels find their inner magnetism
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 03 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02572-z

Hydrogels containing C=O groups and calcium cations show an unexpected paramagnetic effect that may have biomedical applications.
item.dc:title: Hydrogels find their inner magnetism
item.dc:creator: Stephen J. Blundell

item.title: Nanophotonic chip-space interfaces for multidimensional nonlinear optics
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 03 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02570-1

Reconfigurable chip-to-free-space interfaces based on thin-film lithium niobate microrings generate on-demand nonlinear structured light, including tunable optical vortices, optical skyrmions and spatiotemporal vortex pulses.
item.dc:title: Nanophotonic chip-space interfaces for multidimensional nonlinear optics
item.dc:creator: Dunzhao Wei

item.title: Static magnetization switching in an artificial antiferromagnetic multilayer driven by a voltage-controlled magnetic anisotropy effect
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 03 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02575-w

For conventional voltage-controlled magnetic anisotropy (VCMA) switching, a small variation in the pulse widths of the order of several picoseconds can cause switching failure. Now, VCMA-driven static magnetization switching is demonstrated, which is largely insensitive to pulse widths within certain thresholds.
item.dc:title: Static magnetization switching in an artificial antiferromagnetic multilayer driven by a voltage-controlled magnetic anisotropy effect
item.dc:creator: Hiroyasu Nakayama
Takayuki Nozaki
Toshiki Yamaji
Tomohiro Nozaki
Hiroshi Imamura
Shinji Yuasa
